title: Isertia pittieri
taxonomy: Isertia pittieri is classified in the family Rubiaceae, which is a large and diverse family of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family or coffee family. This classification is based on its morphological and genetic characteristics. Isertia pittieri is a member of the genus Isertia, which is a group of flowering plants that are native to Central and South America. The species is further classified as a member of the tribe Psychotrieae, which includes other similar plants. The classification of Isertia pittieri is an essential part of understanding its evolution and relationship to other plants in the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Isertia pittieri is reported to have various medicinal uses, including the treatment of respiratory issues and fever.
